name: Launch Sprint
headline: Use a guided setup lane to create cash flow and faster activation
idealFor: Best for buyers who want implementation before self-serve
description: Launch Sprint bridges product promise and paid execution. It packages setup, positioning, workflow implementation, and first-launch delivery into a tighter premium service lane.
bullets: Stand up the first public funnel, messaging, and workflow system
Reduce time to value for buyers who need more than software access
Create premium service revenue while improving long-term product adoption

deliverables.title: Offer and funnel setup
deliverables.description: Clarify the buyer lane, page structure, and conversion path before the build work starts.

deliverables.title: Implementation mapping
deliverables.description: Translate the work into live pages, workflows, and operator structure instead of leaving the buyer with theory.

deliverables.title: Activation handoff
deliverables.description: Leave the buyer with a stronger system and a cleaner path into software usage.

timeline.title: Scope the brief
timeline.description: Collect the commercial, operational, and launch outcomes that matter most.

timeline.title: Build the first layer
timeline.description: Stand up the first public pages, routing logic, and workflow system.

timeline.title: Prepare the next move
timeline.description: Hand off a cleaner software path, stronger proof, and a better operating baseline.

proofPoints.title: Lead-form-first by design
proofPoints.description: This lane closes best when the buyer can explain the actual job to be done before scope is finalized.

proofPoints.title: Implementation now, software expansion later
proofPoints.description: Launch Sprint creates immediate revenue while strengthening the product path that follows.

sections.title: Done-with-you setup
sections.description: Guide the buyer through positioning, offer structure, workflow mapping, and execution.

sections.title: Faster activation
sections.description: A guided sprint lowers friction for teams that would otherwise stall during setup.

sections.title: Stronger downstream retention
sections.description: Better implementation raises the odds of long-term product usage and deeper service work.

leadForm.heading: Submit the implementation brief for Launch Sprint
leadForm.intro: Describe the launch goals, workflow needs, and delivery constraints so the sprint can be scoped properly.

cta.heading: Use Launch Sprint when the buyer wants the system built with them.
cta.body: This lane is for high-intent buyers who want speed, clarity, and less guesswork before they self-serve.
